title: Integrate agentchattr Chat System (Deep Integration)
description:
Port agentchattr's real-time multi-agent chat features natively into the Moe daemon (TypeScript). Adds channels, @mention routing with loop guards, per-agent read cursors, presence tracking, and task-linked auto-channels. Includes MCP tools for agents, WebSocket protocol extensions, and chat UI in both VS Code and JetBrains plugins.

What we port: Channels, @mention routing, loop guards, per-agent read cursors, presence tracking.
What we skip: Terminal wrapper (Moe uses MCP), decisions (overlaps with proposals), avatar hats, voice typing, image attachments.

Reference: https://github.com/bcurts/agentchattr
architectureNotes:
## Storage Design
- `.moe/channels/{channel-id}.json` — Channel metadata (individual JSON files)
- `.moe/messages/{channel-id}.jsonl` — Append-only JSONL per channel (proven pattern from agentchattr)
- Worker chatCursors stored in existing `.moe/workers/{worker-id}.json`

## Key Patterns
- Messages use JSONL for high-volume append-only writes
- Channels use individual JSON files (infrequent changes)
- Auto-channels created per epic/task with linkedEntityId for reverse lookup
- @mention routing with per-channel hop counters and configurable max hops
- Long-poll `chat_wait` follows existing `waitForTask` pattern
- Plugin fetches messages on-demand per channel (not included in STATE_SNAPSHOT)

## Dependencies Between Tasks
- Task 1 (Schema) must complete before Tasks 2-6
- Task 2 (StateManager) must complete before Tasks 3-6
- Task 3 (Router) must complete before Task 4 (MCP tools use it)
- Task 6 (WebSocket) should complete before Tasks 7-8 (plugin UI)
- Task 9 (Migration) can be done in parallel with Tasks 3-6
epicRails: All new MCP tools must follow existing tool patterns in packages/moe-daemon/src/tools/
Messages stored as JSONL (append-only) per channel for performance
Chat UI in VS Code follows BoardViewProvider.ts patterns
Chat UI in JetBrains follows ActivityLogPanel/ProposalPanel Swing patterns
Per-channel loop guards to prevent runaway agent conversations (default max 4 hops)
Role-based channels (#general, #workers, #architects, #qa) created on project init — no per-task/epic auto-channels
No Python dependency - everything ported to TypeScript
Primary IDE is PyCharm/JetBrains — implement and test JetBrains plugin changes FIRST, VS Code second. JetBrains quality must never be lower than VS Code.
